  1. Replication: Automated, incremental synchronization between central and edge registries or active-standby clusters.
  1. Audit: Comprehensive logging of all artifact operations (pull/push/delete) for security compliance and traceability.

Delivery

Downloading terabyte-sized model weights directly from the origin introduces bandwidth bottlenecks. We utilize Dragonfly for P2P-based distribution, integrated with Harbor for preheating.

Dragonfly P2P-based distribution

For large-scale distribution scenarios, Dragonfly has been deeply optimized based on P2P technology. Taking the example of 500 nodes downloading a 1TB model, the system distributes the initial download tasks of different layers across nodes to maximize downstream bandwidth utilization and avoid single-point congestion. Combined with a secondary bandwidth-aware scheduling algorithm, it dynamically adjusts download paths to eliminate network hotspots and long-tail latency. For individual model weight, Dragonfly splits individual model weights into pieces and fetches them concurrently from the origin. This enables streaming-based downloading, allowing users to share models without waiting for the complete file. This solution has been proven in high-performance AI clusters, utilizing 70%–80% of each node’s bandwidth and improving model deployment efficiency.

Preheating

For latency-sensitive inference services, Harbor triggers Dragonfly to distribute and cache data on target nodes before service scaling. When the instance starts, the model loads from the local disk, achieving zero network latency.

Deployment

Deployment focuses on decoupling the Model (Data) from the Inference Engine (Compute). By leveraging Kubernetes declarative primitives, the Engine runs as a Container, while the Model is mounted as a Volume. This native approach not only enables multiple Pods on the same node to share and reuse the model, saving disk space, but also leverages the preheating and P2P capabilities of Harbor & Dragonfly to eliminate the latency of pulling large model weights, significantly improving startup speed.

OCI Volumes (Kubernetes 1.31+)

Native support for mounting OCI artifacts as volumes via CRI-O/containerd. This feature was introduced as alpha in Kubernetes 1.31 (requires enabling the ImageVolume feature gate) and promoted to beta in Kubernetes 1.33 (enabled by default, no feature gate configuration needed). CRI-O specifically enhances this for LLMs by avoiding decompression overhead at mount time by storing layers uncompressed, resulting in superior performance when mounting large model files.

Step 1: Build YAML

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: vllm-cpu-inference
  labels:
    app: vllm
spec:
  containers:
  - name: vllm
    image: openeuler/vllm-cpu:latest
    command:
    - "python3"
    - "-m"
    - "vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server"
    args:
    - "--model"
    - "/models"
    - "--dtype"
    - "float32"                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
    - "--host"
    - "0.0.0.0"
    - "--port"
    - "8000"
    - "--max-model-len"
    - "1024"
    - "--disable-log-requests"
    env:
    - name: VLLM_CPU_KVCACHE_SPACE
      value: "1"
    - name: VLLM_WORKER_MULTIPROC_METHOD
      value: "spawn"
    resources:
      requests:
        memory: "2Gi"
        cpu: "1"
      limits:
        memory: "16Gi"
        cpu: "8"
    volumeMounts:
    - name: model-volume
      mountPath: /models
      readOnly: true
    ports:
    - containerPort: 8000
      protocol: TCP
      name: http
    livenessProbe:
      httpGet:
        path: /health
        port: 8000
      initialDelaySeconds: 60
      periodSeconds: 10
      timeoutSeconds: 5
    readinessProbe:
      httpGet:
        path: /health
        port: 8000
      initialDelaySeconds: 30
      periodSeconds: 5
  volumes:
  - name: model-volume
    image:
      reference: ghcr.io/chlins/qwen2.5-0.5b:v1
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: vllm-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: vllm
  ports:
  - port: 8000
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    name: http
  type: ClusterIP

Step 2: Deploy inference Workload

Step 3: Call Inference Workload

Model CSI Driver

For compatibility with Kubernetes 1.31 and older, we offer the Model CSI Driver as an interim solution to mount and deploy models as volumes. As OCI Volumes are slated for GA in Kubernetes 1.36, shifting to native OCI Volumes is recommended for the long term.

Step 1: Build YAML

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: vllm-cpu-inference
  labels:
    app: vllm
spec:
  containers:
  - name: vllm
    image: openeuler/vllm-cpu:latest
    command:
    - "python3"
    - "-m"
    - "vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server"
    args:
    - "--model"
    - "/models"
    - "--dtype"
    - "float32"
    - "--host"
    - "0.0.0.0"
    - "--port"
    - "8000"
    - "--max-model-len"
    - "1024"
    - "--disable-log-requests"
    env:
    - name: VLLM_CPU_KVCACHE_SPACE
      value: "1"
    - name: VLLM_WORKER_MULTIPROC_METHOD
      value: "spawn"
    resources:
      requests:
        memory: "2Gi"
        cpu: "1"
      limits:
        memory: "16Gi"
        cpu: "8"
    volumeMounts:
    - name: model-volume
      mountPath: /models
      readOnly: true
    ports:
    - containerPort: 8000
      protocol: TCP
      name: http
    livenessProbe:
      httpGet:
        path: /health
        port: 8000
      initialDelaySeconds: 60
      periodSeconds: 10
      timeoutSeconds: 5
    readinessProbe:
      httpGet:
        path: /health
        port: 8000
      initialDelaySeconds: 30
      periodSeconds: 5
  volumes:
  - name: model-volume
    csi:
      driver: model.csi.modelpack.org
      volumeAttributes:
        model.csi.modelpack.org/reference: ghcr.io/chlins/qwen2.5-0.5b:v1
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: vllm-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: vllm
  ports:
  - port: 8000
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    name: http
  type: ClusterIP

Step 2: Deploy Inference Workload

Step 3: Call Inference Workload

Future

  • Enhanced Preheating: Allow models to be preheated to specified nodes and querying cache distribution across nodes for model-aware pod scheduling.
  • Dragonfly RDMA Acceleration: Enable Dragonfly to utilize InfiniBand or RoCE to improve the speed of distribution.
  • Lazy Loading: Implement on-demand downloading of model weights to reduce startup latency.
  • containerd Optimization: Enhance the OCI Volumes implementation to reduce decompression overhead for large layers.
  • Model Security Scanning: Introduce deep scanning capabilities specifically designed for model weights to detect embedded malicious payloads.

Collaborative Projects

  • Kubernetes: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes
  • Harbor: https://github.com/goharbor/harbor
  • Dragonfly: https://github.com/dragonflyoss/dragonfly
  • CRI-O: https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o
  • containerd: https://github.com/containerd/containerd
  • modctl: https://github.com/modelpack/modctl
  • Model CSI Driver: https://github.com/modelpack/model-csi-driver
  • Model Spec: https://github.com/modelpack/model-spec
  • ORAS: https://github.com/oras-project/oras

References

  • Kubernetes – Read Only Volumes Based On OCI Artifacts: https://kubernetes.io/blog/2024/08/16/kubernetes-1-31-image-volume-source/
  • Harbor – AI Model Processor: https://github.com/goharbor/community/blob/main/proposals/new/AI-model-processor.md
  • Dragonfly – Load-Aware Scheduling Algorithm: https://d7y.io/docs/next/operations/deployment/applications/scheduler/#bandwidth-aware-scheduling-algorithm
  • CRI-O – Add OCI Volume/Image Source Support: https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pull/8317
  • containerd – Add OCI/Image Volume Source support: https://github.com/containerd/containerd/pull/10579
】

随着人工智能在各行业的采用加速，组织面临着一个经常被忽视的关键瓶颈，直到它成为一个严重的障碍：大规模可靠地管理和分发大型模型权重文件。模型的权重是连接训练和推理管道的核心工件，但围绕该工件的基础设施通常是事后才想到的。

本文解决了在企业规模管理 AI 模型工件的操作挑战，并介绍了一种云原生解决方案，该解决方案将软件交付最佳实践（版本控制、不变性和 GitOps）引入大型模型文件的世界。

没人谈论的差距 - 直到它破坏了生产

云原生差距：大多数现有的 ML 模型存储方法在设计时并未考虑 Kubernetes 原生交付，因此在软件工件的管理方式和模型工件的管理方式之间留下了重大差距。在 CNCF 生态系统中，ModelPack、ORAS、Harbor 和 Dragonfly 等项目正在探索管理和分发大型工件的互补方法。

如今，企业在 Kubernetes 上运行人工智能基础设施，但其模型工件管理却滞后。软件容器从 OCI 注册表中提取，具有完整的版本控制、安全扫描和回滚支持。相比之下，模型权重通常通过临时脚本下载，在存储桶之间手动复制，或通过不安全的共享文件系统分发。这种差距造成了部署脆弱性、安全风险和大规模运营开销。

当您的模型比整个应用更重时

现代基础模型并不小。单个模型检查点的范围可以从数十 GB 到数 TB。作为参考，量化的 LLaMA-3 70B 模型重量约为 140 GB，而前沿多模模型可以轻松超过 1 TB。这些不是您使用标准 Git 进行版本控制的文件 - 它们需要专用的存储策略、高效的传输协议和仔细的访问控制。

核心挑战是：大规模存储、分发速度和可重复性。团队需要存储多个模型版本，将它们快速分发到跨区域的 GPU 推理节点，并保证任何部署都可以追溯到精确的、不可变的工件。

三种前进路径 - 以及为什么它们都不够

重新思考交付管道：模型应该比 shell 脚本更好

此处描述的方法将 AI 模型权重视为一流的 OCI（开放容器计划）工件，将它们打包在用于应用程序映像的相同容器注册表中。这使得模型交付能够利用容器工具的完整生态系统：安全扫描、签名来源、GitOps 驱动的部署和 Kubernetes 原生拉取。

如果我们以与发送代码相同的方式发送模型会怎么样？

在云原生时代，开发者早已建立了成熟、高效的软件交付范式。

软件交付：

  • 开发：开发人员将代码提交到 Git 存储库，通过分支管理代码更改，并在关键里程碑处使用标签定义版本。
  • 构建：CI/CD 管道进行编译和测试，将输出打包到不可变的容器映像中。
  • 管理和交付：映像存储在容器注册表中。供应链安全（扫描/签名）、RBAC和P2P分发确保安全交付。
  • 部署：DevOps 工程师使用声明性 Kubernetes YAML 来定义所需的状态。容器的生命周期由 Kubernetes 管理。

    • 云原生AI模块删除交货：

  • 开发：算法工程师将权重和配置推送到 Hugging Face Hub，将其视为 Git 存储库。
  • 构建：CI/CD 管道将权重、运行时配置和元数据打包到不可变的模型工件中。
  • 管理和交付：模型工件由工件注册表管理，重用现有的容器基础设施和工具链。
  • 部署：工程师使用 Kubernetes OCI 卷或模型 CSI 驱动程序。模型通过声明性语义作为卷安装到推理容器中，将 AI 模型与推理引擎（vLLM、SGLang 等）解耦。

    • 通过将软件交付范例和供应链思维应用于模型生命周期管理，我们构建了一个精细、高效的系统，解决了在生产中管理和分发 AI 模型的挑战。

    探索管道：四个步骤的构建故事

    构建

    modctl 是一个 CLI 工具，旨在将 AI 模型打包到 OCI 工件中。它标准化了版本控制、存储、分发和部署，确保与云原生生态系统的集成。

    第1步：自动生成模型文件

    在模型目录中运行以下命令以生成定义文件。

    $ modctl 模型文件生成。

    第 2 步：自定义模型文件

    您还可以自定义模型文件的内容。

    # 型号名称（字符串），如 llama3-8b-instruct、gpt2-xl、qwen2-vl-72b-instruct 等。
名称 qwen2.5-0.5b

# 模型架构（字符串），如transformer、cnn、rnn等。
ARCH变压器

# 模型系列（字符串），例如 llama3、gpt2、qwen2 等。
家庭qwen2

# 模型格式（字符串），如onnx、tensorflow、pytorch等。
格式安全张量

# 指定模型配置文件，支持glob路径模式。
配置config.json

# 指定模型配置文件，支持glob路径模式。
配置生成_config.json

# 模型权重，支持glob路径模式。
模型 *.safetensors

# 指定代码，支持glob路径模式。
代码 *.py

    第 3 步：登录 Artifact Registration（Harbor）

    $ modctl login -u 用户名 -p 密码harbor.registry.com

    第 4 步：构建 OCI 工件

    $ modctl build -tharbor.registry.com/models/qwen2.5-0.5b:v1 -f 模型文件。

    构建后会生成模型清单。 ARCH、FAMILY 和 FORMAT 等描述性信息存储在媒体类型为 application/vnd.cncf.model.config.v1+json 的文件中。

    {
    “架构版本”：2，
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
    "artifactType": "application/vnd.cncf.model.manifest.v1+json",
    “配置”：{
        "mediaType": "application/vnd.cncf.model.config.v1+json",
        “摘要”：“sha256：d5815835051dd97d800a03f641ed8162877920e734d3d705b698912602b8c763”，
        “尺寸”：301
    },
    “层”：[
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.cncf.model.weight.v1.raw",
            “摘要”：“sha256：3f907c1a03bf20f20355fe449e18ff3f9de2e49570ffb536f1a32f20c7179808”，
            “尺寸”：4294967296
        },
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.cncf.model.weight.v1.raw",
            “摘要”：“sha256：6d923539c5c208de77146335584252c0b1b81e35c122dd696fe6e04ed03d7411”，
            “尺寸”：5018536960
        },
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.cncf.model.weight.config.v1.raw",
            “摘要”：“sha256：a5378e569c625f7643952fcab30c74f2a84ece52335c292e630f740ac4694146”，
            “尺寸”：106
        },
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.cncf.model.weight.code.v1.raw",
            “摘要”：“sha256：15da0921e8d8f25871e95b8b1fac958fc9caf453bad6f48c881b3d76785b9f9d",
            “尺寸”：394
        },
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.cncf.model.doc.v1.raw",
            “摘要”：“sha256：5e236ec37438b02c01c83d134203a646cb354766ac294e533a308dd8caa3a11e”，
            “尺寸”：23040
        }
    ]
}

    第 5 步：推送

    $ modctl Pushharbor.registry.com/models/qwen2.5-0.5b:v1

    管理

    当前的人工智能基础设施工作流程主要关注模型分发性能，通常忽略模型管理标准。手动复制适用于实验，但在大规模生产中，缺乏统一的版本控制、元数据规范和生命周期管理是很糟糕的做法。作为标准的云原生工件注册表，Harbor 非常适合模型存储，将模型视为推理工件。

    Harbor 通过以下方式标准化 AI 模型管理：

  • 版本控制：模型是具有不可变标签和 Sha256 摘要的 OCI 工件。这保证了确定性的推理环境。同时直观地呈现模型的基本属性、参数配置、显示信息、文件列表等，既降低了未知版本的风险，又实现了模型的完全透明。

item.title: 【Announcing Kubescape 4.0 Enterprise Stability Meets the AI Era】宣布Kubescape 4.0企业稳定迎接AI时代
item.description:
【

We are happy to announce the release of Kubescape 4.0, a milestone bringing enterprise-grade stability and advanced threat detection to open source Kubernetes security. This version focuses on making security more proactive and scalable. It also introduces capabilities that allow AI agents to utilize Kubescape to scan clusters as well as enable security posture scanning for the AI agents themselves.

Runtime Threat Detection Reaches General Availability (GA)

The highlight of this release is the GA of our Runtime Threat Detection. After rigorous testing, we’ve achieved proven stability at scale.

The engine is powered by CEL-based detection rules. These Common Expression Language rules are highly efficient and have direct access to Kubescape Application Profiles, which act as security baselines for your workloads.

Source: Kubescpe.io

Kubescape 4.0 monitors a comprehensive suite of events including:

  • System Interactions: Processes, Linux capabilities, and System calls
  • Connectivity: Network and HTTP events
  • Storage: File system activities

For seamless operations, Rules and RuleBindings are now managed as Kubernetes CRDs. You can export alerts to your existing stack, including AlertManager, SIEM, Syslog, Stdout, and HTTP webhooks.

Check out the Kubescape documentation for more information.

Kubescape Storage Reaches General Availability (GA)

Kubescape Storage has officially reached GA. This component leverages the Kubernetes Aggregated API, a Kubernetes-native feature, to act as a centralized repository for all security metadata.

By moving custom objects like Application Profiles, SBOMs, and vulnerability manifests into this dedicated storage layer, we’ve ensured that security data doesn’t overwhelm the standard etcd instance. This architecture has been proven to handle the demands of large-scale, high-density clusters, providing the performance required for modern enterprise environments.

For more information, check out Amir Malka’s session at  Kubecon + CloudNativeCon North America 2025:
Extending Kubernetes API: The Hidden Power of Aggregated Server Objects – Amir Malka, ARMO

The Enhanced Node-Agent and Host-Sensor Deprecation

Based on community feedback regarding the complexity of node scanning, we have removed the host-sensor in Kubescape 4.0. While effective, this “pop-up” DaemonSet approach was often perceived as intrusive and difficult to monitor from a security perspective.

We have also officially removed the host-agent and integrated its capabilities directly into the node-agent. By establishing a direct API between the core Kubescape microservices and the node-agent, we’ve eliminated the need for ephemeral, high-privilege Pods. This architectural shift allows you to maintain a cleaner cluster environment with only one agent to manage, making your security posture both more stable and easier to audit.

Kubescape Enters the AI Era

With the launch of Kubescape 4.0, we are addressing the unique challenges of the AI-native era by looking at security from two equally important perspectives. This focus is critical, as the same cloud native principles that scale modern infrastructure are foundational for the next generation of inference pipelines and intelligent, agentic AI systems. We like to think of this as the “two sides of the AI security coin”: using Kubescape to empower AI agents with cybersecurity capabilities and using Kubescape to secure those same agents.

Empowering AI Security Sidekicks

As AI inference becomes the next major cloud native workload and Kubernetes evolves into the platform for intelligent systems, Kubescape 4.0 introduces a KAgent-native plug-in, allowing AI assistants to analyze Kubernetes security posture directly from the cluster. This plug-in provides the following capabilities to the AI agent:

  • Security Scanning: AI agents can list and inspect vulnerability manifests for CVEs and review configuration scans to identify RBAC issues or missing security contexts.
  • Detailed Remediation: Agents can pull specific guidance to fix vulnerabilities.
  • Runtime Observability: Using ApplicationProfiles and NetworkNeighborhoods, AI assistants can look at how containers behave in real life, like what system calls they make, what files they access, and how they communicate over the network.

This integration enables an AI agent to become a true security sidekick; assisting humans to interpret complex security states and make informed decisions.

Scanning the AI Posture

AI agents are beginning to gain more autonomy, meaning their infrastructure must be secured. We need robust security guardrails to stop agents from exploiting them for high-risk actions like unauthorized access or deleting production data. Kubescape 4.0 introduces security posture scanning specifically for KAgent, the CNCF Sandbox project for AI orchestration.

Since KAgent creates direct pathways between AI models and enterprise infrastructure, misconfigurations can be high-risk. Our new analysis identifies 42 security-critical configuration points across KAgent’s CRDs. We are introducing 15 Rego-based controls to detect issues such as:

  • Empty security contexts in default deployments
  • Missing NetworkPolicies
  • Over-privileged controller-wide namespace watching

By applying these rigorous standards, we are ensuring that the “brains” of your AI operations are as secure as the workloads they manage.

Compliance

In the continuously evolving cloud native landscape, robust governance and consistent, auditable compliance are the critical foundations that allow for safe and sustainable innovation. Kubescape continues to help keep your clusters compliant with the latest industry standards:

  • CIS Benchmark Updates: Support for versions 1.12 (Vanilla Kubernetes) and 1.8 (EKS, AKS).


Community Corner

We’d like to welcome our new maintainer, Amir Malka, and thank our emeritus maintainers, David Wertenteil and Craig Box, for their contributions over the years.

To join the Kubescape community and find information on how you can ask questions, join in the conversation, and contribute, visit the link here.

If you are a Kubescape user, we’d love to hear from you. Please reach out if you would like to share an interesting use case with the community or add yourself to our list of adopters.

】

我们很高兴地宣布发布 Kubescape 4.0，这是一个里程碑，为开源 Kubernetes 安全带来了企业级稳定性和高级威胁检测。该版本的重点是使安全性更加主动和可扩展。它还引入了允许 AI 代理利用 Kubescape 扫描集群以及为 AI 代理本身启用安全态势扫描的功能。

运行时威胁检测全面可用 (GA)

此版本的亮点是我们的运行时威胁检测的 GA。经过严格的测试，我们已经实现了经过验证的大规模稳定性。

该引擎由基于 CEL 的检测规则提供支持。这些通用表达式语言规则非常高效，并且可以直接访问 Kubescape 应用程序配置文件，这些配置文件充当您的工作负载的安全基线。

来源：Kubescpe.io

Kubescape 4.0 监控一系列全面的事件，包括：

  • 系统交互：进程、Linux 功能和系统调用
  • 连接性：网络和 HTTP 事件
  • 存储：文件系统活动

为了实现无缝操作，规则和规则绑定现在作为 Kubernetes CRD 进行管理。您可以将警报导出到现有堆栈，包括 AlertManager、SIEM、Syslog、Stdout 和 HTTP Webhook。

查看 Kubescape 文档了解更多信息。

Kubescape 存储全面可用 (GA)

Kubescape Storage 已正式上市。该组件利用 Kubernetes 原生功能 Kubernetes 聚合 API 充当所有安全元数据的集中存储库。

通过移动应用程序配置文件、SBOM 等自定义对象d 漏洞体现在这个专用存储层中，我们确保安全数据不会淹没标准 etcd 实例。事实证明，该架构可以满足大规模、高密度集群的需求，提供现代企业环境所需的性能。

有关详细信息，请查看 Amir Malka 在 Kubecon + CloudNativeCon North America 2025 会议上的会议：
扩展 Kubernetes API：聚合服务器对象的隐藏力量 – Amir Malka，ARMO

弃用增强型节点代理和主机传感器

根据社区对节点扫描复杂性的反馈，我们删除了 Kubescape 4.0 中的主机传感器。虽然有效，但这种“弹出式”DaemonSet 方法通常被认为是侵入性的，并且从安全角度难以监控。

我们还正式删除了主机代理并将其功能直接集成到节点代理中。通过在核心 Kubescape 微服务和节点代理之间建立直接 API，我们消除了对临时、高权限 Pod 的需求。这一架构转变使您可以维护一个更干净的集群环境，只需管理一个代理，从而使您的安全状况更加稳定且更易于审核。

Kubescape进入AI时代

随着 Kubescape 4.0 的推出，我们从两个同样重要的角度看待安全性，以应对 AI 原生时代的独特挑战。这种关注至关重要，因为扩展现代基础设施的云原生原则是下一代推理管道和智能、代理人工智能系统的基础。我们喜欢将其视为“人工智能安全硬币的两面”：使用 Kubescape 为人工智能代理提供网络安全功能，并使用 Kubescape 保护这些代理的安全。

为人工智能安全助手提供支持

随着人工智能推理成为下一个主要的云原生工作负载以及 Kubernetes 演变成智能系统平台，Kubescape 4.0 引入了 KAgent 原生插件，允许人工智能助手直接从集群分析 Kubernetes 安全态势。该插件为AI代理提供以下功能：

  • 安全扫描：AI 代理可以列出和检查 CVE 的漏洞清单，并审查配置扫描以识别 RBAC 问题或缺失的安全上下文。
  • 详细修复：代理可以提取具体指南来修复漏洞。
  • 运行时可观察性：使用 Applic通过 ationProfiles 和 NetworkNeighborhoods，人工智能助手可以查看容器在现实生活中的行为方式，例如它们进行哪些系统调用、它们访问哪些文件以及它们如何通过网络进行通信。

这种集成使人工智能代理成为真正的安全助手；帮助人类解释复杂的安全状态并做出明智的决策。

扫描AI姿势

人工智能代理开始获得更多自主权，这意味着他们的基础设施必须得到保护。我们需要强大的安全护栏来阻止代理利用它们进行高风险操作，例如未经授权的访问或删除生产数据。 Kubescape 4.0 专门针对 KAgent（用于 AI 编排的 CNCF 沙盒项目）引入了安全态势扫描。

由于 KAgent 在 AI 模型和企业基础设施之间创建直接路径，因此错误配置可能会带来高风险。我们的新分析确定了 KAgent CRD 中的 42 个安全关键配置点。我们引入了 15 个基于 Rego 的控件来检测以下问题：

  • 默认部署中的空安全上下文
  • 缺少网络策略
  • 过度特权的控制器范围命名空间监视

通过应用这些严格的标准，我们确保您的 AI 操作的“大脑”与其管理的工作负载一样安全。

合规

在不断发展的云原生环境中，稳健的治理和一致、可审核的合规性是实现安全和可持续创新的关键基础。 Kubescape 继续帮助您的集群符合最新的行业标准：

  • CIS 基准更新：支持版本1.12（Vanilla Kubernetes）和1.8（EKS、AKS）。


社区角

我们欢迎我们的新维护者 Amir Malka，并感谢我们的荣誉维护者 David Wertenteil 和 Craig Box 多年来的贡献。

要加入 Kubescape 社区并查找有关如何提出问题、参与对话和贡献的信息，请访问链接 此处。

如果您是 Kubescape 用户，我们很乐意听取您的意见。如果您想与社区分享有趣的用例或将自己添加到我们的采用者列表中，请与我们联系。

item.title: 【The platform under the model: How cloud native powers AI engineering in production】该模型下的平台：云原生如何为生产中的人工智能工程提供动力
item.description:
【

AI workloads are increasingly running on Kubernetes in production, but for many teams, the path from a working model to a reliable system remains unclear. The cloud native ecosystem – its projects, patterns, and community – offers a growing set of building blocks that help teams bridge these two worlds.

From model to systems

AI Engineering is the discipline of building reliable, production-grade systems that use AI models as components. It goes beyond model training and prompt design into the operational challenges that teams running inference at scale will recognize: serving models with low latency and high availability, efficiently scheduling GPU and accelerator resources, observing token throughput and cost alongside traditional infrastructure metrics, managing model versions and rollouts safely, and enforcing governance and access policies across multi-tenant environments.

These are infrastructure problems, and they map closely to capabilities the cloud native ecosystem has been developing for years.

The cloud native stack for (Gen) AI

If you’re a platform engineer or SRE being asked to support AI workloads, the good news is that much of what you need already exists in the CNCF landscape.

Orchestration and scheduling: Kubernetes is the orchestration layer for AI inference and training. The 2025 CNCF Annual Survey found that 82% of container users run Kubernetes in production, and the platform has evolved well beyond stateless web services. A key development is Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA), which reached GA in Kubernetes 1.34. DRA replaces the limitations of device plugins with fine-grained, topology-aware GPU scheduling using CEL-based filtering and declarative ResourceClaims. For teams managing GPU clusters, DRA is a significant step forward.

Inference routing and load balancing: The Gateway API Inference Extension (Inference Gateway), which has reached GA, provides Kubernetes-native APIs for routing inference traffic based on model names, LoRA adapters, and endpoint health. This enables platform teams to serve multiple GenAI workloads on shared model server pools for higher utilization and fewer required accelerators. Building on this work, the newly formed WG AI Gateway is developing standards for AI-specific networking capabilities: token-based rate limiting, semantic routing, payload processing for prompt filtering, and integration patterns for retrieval-augmented generation.

Observability: OpenTelemetry and Prometheus remain essential. AI workloads introduce new metrics: tokens per second, time to first token, queue depth, cache hit rates. All of them need to live alongside traditional infrastructure telemetry. The inference-perf benchmarking tool, part of some metrics standardization effort, reports key LLM performance metrics and integrates with Prometheus to provide a consistent measurement framework across model servers.

ML workflows: Kubeflow has grown into a top-30 CNCF project with hundreds of active contributors, providing the pipeline orchestration, experiment tracking, and model serving components that ML teams need. Kueue handles job queuing and fair scheduling for batch and training workloads.

Policy and security. Open Policy Agent (OPA) and SPIFFE/SPIRE provide the governance primitives that production AI deployments require, from controlling which teams can access which models to establishing workload identity across inference services.

GitOps and deployment. Argo and Flux bring the same declarative, version-controlled deployment patterns to model serving that platform teams already use for application delivery. Safe rollouts matter even more when a bad model version can produce incorrect or harmful outputs.

Bridging the gap

There’s a real gap between AI practitioners and cloud native practitioners today. Despite their infrastructure-heavy workloads, only 41% of professional AI developers currently identify as cloud native, according to the CNCF and SlashData State of Cloud Native Development report. Many come from data science backgrounds where managed notebook environments abstracted away operational concerns. Meanwhile, cloud native practitioners sometimes view AI workloads as architecturally foreign with stateful, GPU-hungry, and different from the services Kubernetes was originally designed for.

Both perspectives contain some truth, and both communities benefit from closing this gap.

If you’re an AI engineer moving to Kubernetes, start with the inference serving stack. Deploy a model server (vLLM or similar) behind the Inference Gateway, use DRA to manage your GPU resources declaratively, and instrument with OpenTelemetry from the start. The patterns will feel familiar if you’ve worked with any request-response service at scale.

If you’re a platform engineer supporting AI teams, understand the new workload patterns. Inference services need autoscaling based on token throughput, not just CPU. Training jobs are long-running and may span multiple nodes with specialized interconnects. Model artifacts are large and benefit from caching strategies. The CNCF Platform Engineering Maturity Model provides a useful framework for building self-service golden paths that include AI capabilities.

I know, it is easier said than done. And I’m not saying that is just a simple shift from A to B. AI, in which way every, feels the same to what we know so far, but it is on its own also different. With a different complexity, scale and behaviour. As an engineer, I find this exciting. Something new to the ever-same application demands.

Why open source matters here

AI systems are becoming critical infrastructure. The 2025 CNCF Annual Survey found that the top challenge organizations face in cloud native adoption is now cultural, not technical. Team dynamics and leadership alignment outpace tool complexity and training as blockers. This signals maturity: the technology works, and the hard problems are organizational.

For AI infrastructure specifically, open source and vendor-neutral governance provide three things that proprietary stacks cannot easily replicate:

Composability. No single project solves the full AI production stack. It requires a container runtime, a scheduler, a policy engine, an observability pipeline, a workflow orchestrator, an inference gateway, and model serving frameworks, all composed together. The CNCF landscape enables this composition through interoperability and shared standards.

Portability. Organizations are running AI workloads across hyperscalers, GPU-focused cloud providers, and on-premises infrastructure. Kubernetes and the cloud native ecosystem provide the abstraction layer that prevents lock-in to any single provider’s AI stack.

Community-driven evolution. The speed at which the Kubernetes community has responded to AI workload requirements demonstrates how open governance enables rapid adaptation. DRA, different AI-focused work groups, the Inference Gateway, the AI Gateway, the AI Conformance Program…  These are not top-down product decisions; they’re community responses to real practitioner needs, shaped through KEPs, working groups, and public design discussions.

Getting started

If you want to explore the intersection of cloud native and AI Engineering, here are some entry points:

  • Try the Inference Gateway: The getting started guide walks you through deploying an inference-aware load balancer on your cluster.
  • Explore DRA for GPU scheduling: The Kubernetes documentation on DRA covers the concepts and API objects. Start by understanding ResourceClaims and DeviceClasses.
  • Join the community: There are multiple active work groups developing proposals for new AI standards. The #wg-ai-gateway channel on Kubernetes Slack is a good starting point.
  • Attend KubeCon + CloudNativeCon: The AI tracks and co-located events provide hands-on exposure to these patterns and a chance to connect with practitioners solving the same problems.

Looking ahead

The cloud native ecosystem didn’t emerge specifically for AI, but it is increasingly well-suited to supporting AI systems in production. The model may drive innovation, but the platform determines how reliably that innovation reaches users.

Much of the important work ahead sits at the intersection of these communities. Whether you’re building models or platforms, there is an opportunity to shape how AI systems are operated in practice, through open collaboration, shared standards, and real-world experience.

】

人工智能工作负载越来越多地在生产中运行在 Kubernetes 上，但对于许多团队来说，从工作模型到可靠系统的路径仍不清楚。云原生生态系统（其项目、模式和社区）提供了一组不断增长的构建块，帮助团队在这两个世界之间架起桥梁。

从模型到系统

人工智能工程是构建使用人工智能模型作为组件的可靠、生产级系统的学科。它超越了模型训练和提示设计，解决了大规模运行推理的团队将认识到的运营挑战：以低延迟和高可用性提供模型，高效调度 GPU 和加速器资源，观察令牌吞吐量和成本以及传统基础设施指标，安全管理模型版本和部署，以及跨多租户环境实施治理和访问策略。

这些都是基础设施问题，它们与云原生生态系统多年来开发的功能密切相关。

（Gen）AI 的云原生堆栈

如果您是平台工程师或 SRE，被要求支持 AI 工作负载，那么好消息是 CNCF 环境中已经存在您所需的大部分内容。

编排和调度：Kubernetes 是 AI 推理和训练的编排层。 2025 CNCF 年度调查发现，82% 的容器用户在生产中运行 Kubernetes，并且该平台的发展已经远远超出了无状态 Web 服务的范围。一个关键的发展是动态资源分配（DRA），它在 Kubernetes 1.34 中达到了 GA。 DRA 使用基于 CEL 的过滤和声明性 ResourceClaims 进行细粒度、拓扑感知的 GPU 调度，从而取代了设备插件的限制。对于管理 GPU 集群的团队来说，DRA 是向前迈出的重要一步。

推理路由和负载平衡：网关 API 推理扩展（推理网关）已达到 GA 版本，提供 Kubernetes 原生 API，用于根据模型名称、LoRA 适配器和端点运行状况路由推理流量。这使得平台团队能够在共享模型服务器池上为多个 GenAI 工作负载提供服务，以提高利用率并减少所需的加速器。在此工作的基础上，新成立的 WG AI Gateway 正在开发 AI 特定网络功能的标准：基于令牌的速率限制、语义路由、用于提示过滤的负载处理以及用于检索增强生成的集成模式。

可观察性：OpenTelemetry 和 Prometheus 仍然至关重要。人工智能工作负载引入了新的指标：每秒令牌数、第一个令牌的时间、队列深度、缓存命中率。所有这些都需要与传统基础设施遥测共存。 inference-perf 基准测试工具是某些指标标准化工作的一部分，它报告关键的 LLM 性能指标并与 Prometheus 集成，以在模型服务器之间提供一致的测量框架。

ML 工作流程： Kubeflow 已发展成为拥有数百名活跃贡献者的前 30 个 CNCF 项目，提供 ML 团队所需的管道编排、实验跟踪和模型服务组件。 Kueue 处理批处理和训练工作负载的作业排队和公平调度。

策略和安全性。开放策略代理 (OPA) 和 SPIFFE/SPIRE 提供生产 AI 部署所需的治理原语，从控制哪些团队可以访问哪些模型到跨推理服务建立工作负载身份。

GitOps 和部署。Argo 和 Flux 为平台团队已经用于应用程序交付的模型服务带来了相同的声明式、版本控制的部署模式。当错误的模型版本可能产生不正确或有害的输出时，安全推出就更加重要。

缩小差距

如今，人工智能从业者和云原生从业者之间确实存在差距。根据 CNCF 和 SlashData 云原生开发状态报告，尽管基础设施工作负载繁重，但目前只有 41% 的专业 AI 开发人员认为自己是云原生。许多人具有数据科学背景，其中托管笔记本环境抽象了操作问题。与此同时，云原生从业者有时将 AI 工作负载视为架构上的异类，具有状态性、需要 GPU，并且与 Kubernetes 最初设计的服务不同。

两种观点都有一定的道理，两个社区都可以从缩小这一差距中受益。

如果您是转向 Kubernetes 的 AI 工程师，请从推理服务堆栈开始。在推理网关后面部署模型服务器（vLLM 或类似服务器），使用 DRA 以声明方式管理 GPU 资源，并从一开始就使用 OpenTelemetry 进行检测。如果您曾大规模使用过任何请求-响应服务，那么这些模式会让您感到熟悉。

如果您是支持 AI 团队的平台工程师，请了解新的工作负载模式。推理服务需要根据令牌吞吐量（而不仅仅是 CPU）进行自动缩放。训练作业需要长时间运行，并且可能跨越多个节点具有专门的互连。模型工件很大并且受益于缓存策略。 CNCF 平台工程成熟度模型为构建包含 AI 功能的自助黄金路径提供了一个有用的框架。

我知道，说起来容易做起来难。我并不是说这只是从 A 到 B 的简单转变。人工智能在某种程度上与我们迄今为止所知道的一样，但它本身也有所不同。具有不同的复杂性、规模和行为。作为一名工程师，我觉得这令人兴奋。相同的应用程序需求中有一些新的东西。

为什么开源在这里很重要

人工智能系统正在成为关键的基础设施。 2025 CNCF 年度调查发现，组织在采用云原生方面面临的最大挑战现在是文化，而不是技术。团队动力和领导力一致性超过了工具复杂性和培训作为阻碍因素。这标志着成熟：技术有效，而难题是组织性的。

特别是对于人工智能基础设施而言，开源和供应商中立的治理提供了专有堆栈无法轻松复制的三件事：

可组合性。没有一个项目能够解决完整的人工智能生产堆栈。它需要容器运行时、调度程序、策略引擎、可观察性管道、工作流编排器、推理网关和模型服务框架，所有这些都组合在一起。 CNCF 格局通过互操作性和共享标准实现了这种组合。

可移植性。组织正在跨超大规模企业、以 GPU 为中心的云提供商和本地基础设施运行 AI 工作负载。 Kubernetes 和云原生生态系统提供了抽象层，可防止锁定任何单一提供商的 AI 堆栈。

社区驱动的演进。Kubernetes 社区对 AI 工作负载需求的响应速度展示了开放治理如何实现快速适应。 DRA、不同的以人工智能为中心的工作组、推理网关、人工智能网关、人工智能一致性计划……这些都不是自上而下的产品决策；它们是社区对实际从业者需求的回应，通过 KEP、工作组和公共设计讨论形成。

开始使用

如果您想探索云原生和人工智能工程的交叉点，这里有一些切入点：

  • 尝试推理网关：入门指南将引导您在集群上部署推理感知负载均衡器。
  • 探索 DRA f或 GPU 调度：DRA 上的 Kubernetes 文档涵盖了概念和 API 对象。首先了解 ResourceClaims 和 DeviceClasses。
  • 加入社区：有多个活跃的工作组正在为新的人工智能标准制定提案。 Kubernetes Slack 上的 #wg-ai-gateway 频道是一个很好的起点。
  • 参加 KubeCon + CloudNativeCon：AI 专题和同期举办的活动提供了亲身接触这些模式的机会，并有机会与解决相同问题的从业者进行交流。

展望未来

云原生生态系统并不是专门针对人工智能而出现的，但它越来越适合支持生产中的人工智能系统。该模型可能会推动创新，但平台决定了创新到达用户的可靠性。

未来的许多重要工作都位于这些社区的交汇处。无论您是构建模型还是平台，都有机会通过开放协作、共享标准和现实经验来塑造人工智能系统在实践中的运作方式。

item.title: 【Higress Joins CNCF: Delivering an enterprise-grade AI gateway and a seamless path from Nginx Ingress】Higress 加入 CNCF：提供企业级 AI 网关和 Nginx Ingress 的无缝路径
item.description:
【

We are thrilled to announce that Higress has officially passed the Technical Oversight Committee (TOC) vote to join the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) as a Sandbox project, becoming a proud member of the CNCF ecosystem.

What is Higress?

Higress is an AI-native, high-performance API gateway built on Envoy and Istio. It conceptually unifies the traffic gateway, microservices gateway, and AI gateway into a single control plane, drastically reducing operational complexity for both cloud-native and AI workloads.

Born out of Alibaba’s massive-scale internal production environments, Higress brings enterprise-grade stability and WebAssembly (Wasm) extensibility to the CNCF ecosystem. Today, its core capabilities are built upon two main pillars:

  • Ingress controller and gateway capabilities: Higress serves as a highly mature Kubernetes Ingress Controller, offering full support for the Gateway API and its Inference Extension. With Nginx Ingress scheduled for retirement in 2026[1], Higress provides a secure, drop-in replacement for Nginx Ingress. It remains fully compatible with mainstream Nginx Ingress annotations while replacing the vulnerable configuration-injection model with a robust xDS control plane and Wasm sandbox, effectively eliminating the security risks inherent to legacy architectures. Whether organizations choose to maintain existing Ingress setups or migrate fully to the Gateway API, Higress delivers unified, highly scalable traffic governance.
  • AI-native gateway capabilities: Higress treats AI traffic as a first-class citizen. It provides native support for Large Language Model (LLM) invocations, the Model Context Protocol (MCP), and diverse AI inference scenarios. By offering token-based rate limiting, multi-model fallback, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) integration, model-aware routing, and intelligent load balancing, Higress standardizes how cloud-native applications consume LLMs. This positions Higress as the definitive entry point for AI Agents and LLM traffic.

Customer adoption

Higress has already proven its production-ready reliability across a wide range of highly demanding environments. Current enterprise adopters include Alibaba Group, Ant Group, BOSS Zhipin, Cathay Insurance, Ctrip, DJI, Kuaishou, Sealos, and Vipshop. These organizations span major industries such as internet services, finance, travel, hardware, and entertainment. They leverage Higress not only for its robust cloud-native traffic routing but also for deploying AI gateway features directly to enterprise AI Agent applications and MCP services. Furthermore, a growing ecosystem of innovative AI open-source projects, including HiMarket and HiClaw, are already being built on top of Higress.

Why CNCF?

Higress’s entry into the CNCF marks a pivotal milestone in its evolutionary journey, driven by three core objectives:

  1. Deep ecosystem alignment: The CNCF is home to foundational open-source projects like Kubernetes and Envoy. Built on Envoy and Istio, Higress shares a technical heritage that is inherently native to this environment. Joining the CNCF allows Higress to collaborate intimately with these top-tier projects, co-create technical standards, and solidify its leading role in modern cloud-native architectures.
  2. Neutral, community-driven growth: The long-term vitality of any open-source project relies on a diverse, active contributor base. By leveraging the CNCF’s neutral governance framework, Higress can attract developers, users, and enterprise contributors from across the globe, free from single-vendor influence. This open model will accelerate innovation and ensure the project remains agile in meeting world wide user needs.
  3. Standardizing AI infrastructure: As AI applications explode in popularity, the market desperately needs infrastructure explicitly optimized for AI workloads. With its advanced capabilities in unified multi-model management and AI Agent integration, Higress is well-positioned to drive the establishment of universal AI gateway standards within the CNCF platform.

Roadmap: Long-term compatibility and ecosystem evolution

Looking ahead, the project will focus its development on two primary tracks:

  1. Long-term Ingress compatibility, full Gateway API and Inference Extension support, and comprehensive Nginx Ingress migration solutions:
  • Maintain deep compatibility with standard Ingress and Nginx Ingress annotations while supporting a dual-stack coexistence with the Gateway API.
  • Strengthen the delivery of Gateway API and Inference Extension capabilities, providing sustainable open-source infrastructure for cloud-native networking and AI applications.
  • Deliver comprehensive Nginx Ingress migration paths, ensuring users experience a smooth transition whether they adopt the Gateway API or stick with traditional Ingress.
  1. Continuous AI capability expansion:
  • Deepen support for Model Context Protocol (MCP) to make Higress the standard entry point for AI agents and LLM traffic;
  • Introduce “Higress Agent” for autonomous, intelligent traffic governance.
  • Enhance support for real-time communication protocols critical to AI streaming scenarios.
  • Build robust security management and progressive disclosure frameworks for AI skills.
  • Continue advancing core AI-native traffic features, including token-based rate limiting, multi-model fallback, and intelligent load balancing.

Sandbox is just the beginning

Joining the CNCF Sandbox is a starting point, not a finish line. The Higress community remains deeply committed to continuous investment in transparent governance, high-quality documentation, and global community growth.

We thank the CNCF Technical Oversight Committee, the broader cloud-native community, and every contributor and adopter who has supported Higress. We look forward to building a more secure, scalable, and AI-friendly infrastructure alongside the CNCF ecosystem.

Learn more: higress.ai |GitHub |Documentation

[1] https://kubernetes.io/blog/2026/01/29/ingress-nginx-statement/

】

我们很高兴地宣布 Higress 已正式通过技术监督委员会 (TOC) 投票(CNCF) 作为沙盒项目，成为 CNCF 生态系统的骄傲成员。

什么是 Higress？

Higress 是一个基于 Envoy 和 Istio 构建的人工智能原生高性能 API 网关。它从概念上将流量网关、微服务网关和 AI 网关统一到单个控制平面中，从而大大降低了云原生和 AI 工作负载的操作复杂性。

Higress 脱胎于阿里巴巴大规模的内部生产环境，为 CNCF 生态系统带来了企业级稳定性和 WebAssembly（Wasm）可扩展性。如今，其核心能力建立在两个主要支柱之上：

  • 入口控制器和网关功能：Higress 作为高度成熟的 Kubernetes 入口控制器，为网关 API 及其推理扩展提供全面支持。 Nginx Ingress 计划于 2026 年退役[1]，Higress 为 Nginx Ingress 提供了安全、直接的替代品。它与主流 Nginx Ingress 注释完全兼容，同时用强大的 xDS 控制平面和 Wasm 沙箱替换易受攻击的配置注入模型，有效消除了遗留架构固有的安全风险。无论组织选择维护现有的 Ingress 设置还是完全迁移到网关 API，Higress 都能提供统一、高度可扩展的流量治理。
  • AI 原生网关功能：Higress 将 AI 流量视为一等公民。它为大型语言模型 (LLM) 调用、模型上下文协议 (MCP) 和各种 AI 推理场景提供本机支持。通过提供基于令牌的速率限制、多模型回退、检索增强生成 (RAG) 集成、模型感知路由和智能负载平衡，Higress 标准化了云原生应用程序如何使用 LLM。这使得 Higress 成为人工智能代理和 LLM 流量的最终入口点。

客户采用

Higress 已经在各种高要求环境中证明了其生产就绪的可靠性。目前采用的企业包括阿里巴巴集团、蚂蚁集团、BOSS直聘、国泰保险、携程、大疆、快手、Sealos和唯品会。这些组织横跨互联网服务、金融、旅游、硬件和娱乐等主要行业。他们利用 Higress 不仅是因为其强大的云原生流量路由，而且还可以将 AI 网关功能直接部署到企业 AI 代理应用程序和 MCP 服务。此外，包括 HiMarket 和 HiClaw 在内的不断发展的创新人工智能开源项目生态系统已经建立在 Higress 之上。

为什么选择 CNCF？

Higress 加入 CNCF 标志着其发展历程中的一个关键里程碑，由三个核心目标驱动：

  • 深度生态系统协调：CNCF 是 Kubernetes 和 Envoy 等基础开源项目的所在地。 Higress 建立在 Envoy 和 Istio 的基础上，拥有这种环境固有的技术传统。加入 CNCF 使 Higress 能够与这些顶级项目密切合作，共同制定技术标准，并巩固其在现代云原生架构中的领导地位。
  • 中立、社区驱动的增长：任何开源项目的长期活力都依赖于多元化、活跃的贡献者基础。通过利用 CNCF 的中立治理框架，Higress 可以吸引来自全球的开发者、用户和企业贡献者，而不受单一供应商的影响。这种开放模式将加速创新并确保项目在满足全球用户需求方面保持敏捷。
  • 标准化 AI 基础设施：随着 AI 应用程序的普及，市场迫切需要针对 AI 工作负载进行明确优化的基础设施。凭借其在统一多模型管理和 AI Agent 集成方面的先进能力，Higress 完全有能力推动 CNCF 平台内通用 AI 网关标准的建立。

    • 路线图：长期兼容性和生态系统演进

    展望未来，该项目的发展将集中在两个主要轨道上：

  • 长期 Ingress 兼容性、完整的网关 API 和推理扩展支持以及全面的 Nginx Ingress 迁移解决方案：
    • 保持与标准 Ingress 和 Nginx Ingress 注释的深度兼容性，同时支持与 Gateway API 的双栈共存。
    • 加强网关 API 和推理扩展功能的交付，为云原生网络和 AI 应用提供可持续的开源基础设施。
    • 提供全面的 Nginx Ingress 迁移路径，确保用户无论采用 Gateway API 还是坚持使用传统 Ingress 都能体验到平稳过渡。
    1. 持续扩展人工智能能力：
    • 加深对模型上下文协议 (MCP) 的支持，使 Higress 成为 AI 代理和 LLM 流量的标准入口点；
    • 引入“Higress Agent”以实现自主、智能的流量治理。
    • 增强对 AI 流场景至关重要的实时通信协议的支持。
    • 为 AI 技能构建强大的安全管理和渐进式披露框架。
    • 继续推进核心 AI 原生流量功能，包括基于令牌的速率限制、多模型回退和智能负载平衡。

    沙盒只是开始

    加入 CNCF 沙盒是一个起点，而不是终点线。 Higress 社区仍然坚定地致力于对透明治理、高质量文档和全球社区发展的持续投资。

    我们感谢 CNCF 技术监督委员会、更广泛的云原生社区以及支持 Higress 的每一位贡献者和采用者。我们期待与 CNCF 生态系统一起构建更安全、可扩展、人工智能友好的基础设施。

    了解更多：higress.ai |GitHub |文档

    [1] https://kubernetes.io/blog/2026/01/29/ingress-nginx-statement/
